result: CodexThink core MCP tools not exposed; local fallback recorded
Official Tauri updater, installer, signing, Microsoft process APIs, Epic Unreal references, community reports, reference repositories, Kuro terms and derivative-work guideline reviewed

Architecture, UI, backup/safety, bilingual UI, MIT, prompt-before-install updater, integrated process supervisor, raw editor/diff/custom profiles, all priority classes, and original non-character icon approved
Thirteen-task TDD implementation plan passed requirement coverage, placeholder, interface consistency, JSON, and git diff hygiene review
Created and fetched the Wuwa ini Tool engineering design hub with four focused child pages, architecture/apply/state/release diagrams, DB-none evidence, risks, verification and glossary
Tauri/React foundation passed frontend 1/1, typecheck, Vite build, Rust 2/2, rustfmt and diff checks; reviewer approved after non-Windows unsupported_platform TDD fix

Lossless INI parser and managed merge passed focused 17/17 and full Rust 19/19 tests, clippy -D warnings, rustfmt and diff checks; reviewer approved after UTF-32 BOM and ASCII-only name handling fixes

Bilingual shared contracts, theme tokens and accessible primitives passed focused 20/20 and full frontend 21/21 tests, typecheck, Vite build, Prettier and diff checks; reviewer approved after tooltip state, ARIA merge and WCAG contrast fixes

Steam app 3513350 layout and current native/Steam Client tree evidence support deriving Engine.ini only from a validated Client-Win64-Shipping.exe; LOCALAPPDATA is excluded from automatic writes and native registry entries are candidate hints only

User installation resolves to C:\Program Files\Wuthering Waves\Wuthering Waves Game\Client\Saved\Config\WindowsNoEditor\Engine.ini and reports alternate UserEngine-style configuration is blocked; scope is pinned to the validated Engine.ini leaf without alternate-file bypasses
User requested reliable export/load sharing of custom presets between users; design requires a portable, validated, preview-before-save format with no automatic apply
User requested non-catalog Engine.ini keys such as r.IniruFPSOpti to be addable and preserved across profile save, export, and import
User requested downloading/copying a complete Engine.ini elsewhere and loading or pasting the whole document into the app for guarded application

Evidence-aware catalog, conservative built-ins, validated custom INI entries, revisioned profile storage and portable preview-before-save sharing passed focused 30/30 and full Rust 80/80 tests, clippy -D warnings, rustfmt, JSON parse and diff checks; reviewer approved after trust-boundary, parser-idempotence and evidence-status fixes

Validated Kuro, Steam app 3513350, and manual discovery passed focused 19/19 and full Rust 103/103 tests, clippy -D warnings, rustfmt and diff checks; reviewer approved after Windows-safe installdir, canonical Steam containment, reparse and user-confirmation fixes

User requested an optional WuWa-focused global performance mode while protecting Discord, recording and streaming workloads; Microsoft process/QoS guidance and public System Informer, CPU Affinity Tool, GameOptimizer, MSFS AutoFPS and Process Lasso behavior were compared, with temporary least-privilege priority changes, explicit exclusions and exact restoration selected instead of registry or power-plan tweaks

Byte-exact backup, first-original pinning, stale-preview guard, atomic replacement, metadata restoration, durable recovery journals, ambiguous-state preservation, cleanup warning aggregation, and strict temporary-file scavenging passed focused backup tests 27/27, full Rust tests 128/128, clippy -D warnings, rustfmt and diff checks; independent final review approved with no remaining findings

User requested independent WuWa and NVIDIA shader-cache cleanup; NVIDIA's 2026 support procedure documents current-user DXCache, GLCache and NV_Cache roots and automatic recreation, while recent WuWa community reports identify Client/Saved/PSO and PSOReport but also warn of recompilation and stutter. The design therefore uses separate explicit targets, exact allowlists, preview, no LocalStorage deletion, shared-driver-cache warnings, and partial locked-file reporting

Microsoft CPU Set and QoS documentation, Epic Unreal game/render-thread profiling guidance, public MSFS AutoFPS and Linux GameMode designs, System Informer, and Process Lasso behavior were compared. The selected independent design measures sustained contention, lowers only eligible Normal-priority background work temporarily, uses hard communication/capture exclusions, reports per-core saturation, prefers soft CPU Sets, optionally normalizes only WuWa execution-speed throttling, and restores exact prior state instead of copying persistent optimizer rules

User reported one- or two-core saturation followed by frame drops in WuWa. Epic documentation confirms Unreal frame time can bottleneck on game or render threads while community WuWa reports are mixed, so the app will expose per-core and game-thread CPU-time telemetry and gate soft background CPU Set isolation rather than asserting a universal engine bottleneck or hard-affining the game

The supplied 2026 WuWa performance analysis separates dense-area GameThread pressure from boundary streaming, shader warmup and inferred GC compound hitches; reports measured max-thread saturation with modest aggregate CPU; documents TaskGraph workers; and explicitly lists limitations. The design uses these observations as community evidence for hot-thread diagnostics, safe background headroom, cache-rebuild warnings, and evidence states for streaming/culling options, while rejecting universal causality and automatic mobile-to-PC PoolSize values

Added six source-reviewed streaming and culling options with original bilingual cautions, community/experimental/regressed states, high or medium risk, no runtime-verification claim, and Vanilla-only null removal. Catalog/profile integration tests passed 31/31; JSON, rustfmt and diff checks passed

Implemented validated Windows CPU topology, CPU Sets, single-group hard-affinity compatibility, all six priority classes, readback, durable exact-restore Focus/QoS journals, protected communication/capture/audio families, adaptive aggregate and hot-thread telemetry, hysteresis, and advisory soft headroom. Focus/QoS/process tests passed 40/40, full host Rust passed 176/176, all-target clippy, fmt and diff checks passed, and actual Windows modules passed an isolated MSVC target probe; independent review approved with no Critical or Important findings

Implemented independent WuWa PSO/PSOReport and current-user NVIDIA DXCache/GLCache/NV_Cache preview-confirm cleanup with typed troubleshooting and driver-wide warnings, game-running and mid-operation stop receipts, approved-manifest-only deletion, Windows drive-to-root and descendant no-follow handle pins, stable file identity checks, explicit per-root outcomes, and path-free crash checkpoints capped at 50 with no-replace write-through publication. Cache integration passed 21/21, cache unit tests passed 6/6, all-target clippy/fmt/diff checks and an isolated actual-module MSVC test compile passed; independent two-round review approved with no remaining blocking or important findings
Updated and fetched the project engineering hub to record Tasks 1-7 completion, Task 8 secure supervisor/command work, and the remaining UI/updater/Windows release gates

Implemented the single-instance tray supervisor, restrictive Tauri command surface and CSP, backend-owned native dialogs and external-link allowlist, installation-generation-bound restore and Focus tokens, shared exclusive maintenance/shutdown/update gates, signed-updater approval boundary, fail-closed Windows process observation, adaptive Focus telemetry events, and crash-safe exact restoration. Full Rust all-target tests passed 228/228; all-feature clippy with warnings denied, rustfmt and diff checks passed; independent final review approved with zero Critical, Important, or actionable Minor findings. Actual Windows runtime and bundle behavior remains a release gate.
Updated and fetched the project verification and release log with Tasks 1-8 completion, Task 8 commit and gates, secure runtime boundaries, and Task 9 as the next UI/desktop QA gate

Added bilingual open-source governance, structured issue and PR intake, Dependabot, frontend/Rust/Windows CI, and protected draft release automation with signed updater, checksum, SBOM, and required-asset gates. Draft 2020-12 AJV validation compiled all four schemas and validated 17 options, four INI presets, and three CPU presets. npm ci, frontend 28/28 tests, typecheck, production build, version/catalog policies, YAML parsing, and diff checks passed. Release configuration correctly remains fail-closed until the updater public key, endpoint, artifacts, and Windows icon are committed.
Updated and fetched the verification and release log with Task 12 commit and gates, Draft 2020-12 AJV validation, fail-closed release prerequisites, and Tasks 9–11 as the active gates.

Completed all desktop application surfaces and typed Tauri workflows for discovery, lossless Engine.ini diff/apply/raw import/custom entries, portable profiles, backups, CPU and priority controls, adaptive Focus Mode, separate cache maintenance, bilingual safety states, and settings. Frontend workflow coverage passed 73/73 tests and the independent final review reported zero Critical, Important, or Minor findings.

Integrated an original generated non-character [T] raster mark and deterministic Tauri icon set. The icon policy verified ICO frames at 16, 24, 32, 48, 64, and 256 pixels, and no Kuro character-derived art is shipped.

Completed the global signed-updater experience using one backend-held Update from metadata through install, user approval, mutual-exclusion gates, and actual downloaded/total byte progress serialized losslessly to the frontend. The Tauri config parses with passive Windows install and signed artifact generation; independent review approved with no findings. Windows signature, tamper, interruption, and upgrade behavior remains a release gate.

Frontend 73/73, Rust 242/242, TypeScript typecheck, Vite production build, rustfmt, all-target/all-feature Clippy with warnings denied, version/catalog/icon policies, npm production audit with zero vulnerabilities, Tauri config parse, and git diff hygiene all passed on the macOS host.

Updated and fetched the verification and release log with Tasks 9–11, integration commit a2fe6ed, frontend 73/73 and Rust 242/242 gates, zero-finding final review, signed updater and original brand completion, and the remaining Task 13 Windows release gates.

Published public prerelease v1.0.0-beta.6 after main CI run 29381518275 and signed draft-release run 29381855443 passed. Verified the 4.1 MB NSIS installer packages wuwa-ini-tool.exe, exact GitHub asset names, portable SHA-256 checksums for installer and signature, updater metadata/signature consistency, and valid SPDX JSON SBOM.
